mastery_criteria: Explain that a group is a set with a binary operation satisfying closure, associativity, identity, and inverses.
Distinguish a group from a set that lacks one of the required axioms.
misconceptions: A group is just any collection of objects.
Commutativity is required for every group.
student_response: A group is a set with an operation where you can combine elements and stay in the set, there is an identity element, and each element has an inverse. I think associativity also has to hold, but commutativity is optional.
